questions.questionText: As a Power Platform Solution Architect, what is the first step when designing a solution for a customer?
options: A. Start building canvas apps immediately.
B. Gather and analyze business requirements, including functional and non-functional requirements.
C. Purchase all necessary licenses.
D. Deploy a production environment.
questions.explanation: The first step is understanding the business requirements through discovery sessions, including functional requirements (what the solution must do) and non-functional requirements (performance, security, scalability). Building before understanding requirements leads to rework. Licensing and deployment come after design.

questions.questionText: What is the recommended minimum environment strategy for enterprise Power Platform deployments?
options: A. A single production environment for everything.
B. Separate Development, Test/UAT, and Production environments.
C. Only a development and production environment.
D. Use personal developer environments for production workloads.
questions.explanation: Enterprise deployments should have at minimum Development, Test/UAT, and Production environments to support proper ALM, testing, and controlled deployments. A single environment risks production stability. Skipping testing is risky. Personal environments are not for production workloads.

questions.questionText: Which integration pattern is most appropriate when data must be synchronized between Dataverse and an external system in near real-time?
options: A. Manual file export/import.
B. Event-driven integration using webhooks or service bus.
C. Nightly batch processing only.
D. Printing reports and re-entering data.
questions.explanation: Event-driven integration using webhooks, Azure Service Bus, or similar technologies provides near real-time data synchronization by triggering actions when data changes occur. Manual export/import is labor-intensive. Nightly batches have high latency. Manual re-entry is error-prone.

questions.questionText: In Dataverse, which security model provides the most granular control over which records a user can access?
options: A. Environment-level security only.
B. A combination of security roles, business units, teams, and field-level security.
C. Azure Active Directory conditional access only.
D. Network firewall rules.
questions.explanation: Dataverse provides layered security through security roles (table and record privileges), business units (organizational hierarchy), teams (group-based access), and field-level security (column-level restrictions). Environment security is too coarse. AAD conditional access controls authentication, not data access. Firewalls control network access, not data-level security.

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of Data Loss Prevention (DLP) policies in Power Platform?
options: A. To encrypt data at rest.
B. To classify connectors into business and non-business groups, preventing data flow between categories.
C. To back up Dataverse data.
D. To manage user licenses.
questions.explanation: DLP policies classify connectors into Business, Non-Business, and Blocked groups, preventing connectors in different groups from being used together in the same app or flow. This prevents data leakage. DLP does not encrypt, backup, or manage licenses.

questions.questionText: What does the Center of Excellence (CoE) Starter Kit provide for Power Platform?
options: A. A set of pre-built business applications.
B. A collection of components for governance, monitoring, and nurturing Power Platform adoption in an organization.
C. A code editor for building custom connectors.
D. A migration tool from Salesforce.
questions.explanation: The CoE Starter Kit provides apps, flows, and dashboards for discovering existing Power Platform resources, setting up governance policies, monitoring usage, and nurturing citizen developer adoption. It is not pre-built business apps, a code editor, or a migration tool.

questions.questionText: Who can create environments in Power Platform by default, and how can this be restricted?
options: A. Only global admins can create environments.
B. By default, all licensed users can create trial environments; admins can restrict this in the Power Platform admin center.
C. Environment creation cannot be restricted.
D. Users must submit a support ticket to create environments.
questions.explanation: By default, licensed users can create trial and developer environments. Administrators can restrict who can create environments through tenant-level settings in the Power Platform admin center. It is not limited to global admins by default. Restrictions are available. Support tickets are not required.

questions.topic: Solution Design - Model-Driven vs Canvas Apps
questions.questionText: When should a Solution Architect recommend a model-driven app over a canvas app?
options: A. When the app needs a highly customized, pixel-perfect UI.
B. When the app is data-centric, requires complex business logic, and benefits from Dataverse's security model.
C. When the app has minimal data requirements.
D. When the app must work offline on mobile devices only.
questions.explanation: Model-driven apps are ideal for data-centric applications with complex business rules, relationships, and role-based security needs, as they are built on Dataverse. Canvas apps offer pixel-perfect UI flexibility. Minimal data needs favor canvas apps. Both app types can work on mobile.

questions.questionText: What is the benefit of using virtual tables (virtual entities) in Dataverse?
options: A. They store data faster than regular tables.
B. They provide a real-time view of external data without importing it into Dataverse.
C. They automatically create backup copies.
D. They replace the need for security roles.
questions.explanation: Virtual tables display external data (from SQL Server, SharePoint, OData services, etc.) in Dataverse without copying it, providing a real-time view with standard Dataverse table interfaces. They do not store data (so not faster storage). They do not create backups or replace security roles.

questions.questionText: How does the manager hierarchy security model in Dataverse work?
options: A. It gives all managers admin access.
B. It allows managers to access records owned by their direct and indirect reports, based on the organizational hierarchy.
C. It encrypts data for managers only.
D. It prevents managers from seeing any records.
questions.explanation: The manager hierarchy security model grants read access to records owned by direct and indirect reports in the organizational hierarchy, enabling managers to see their team's data without explicit sharing. It does not grant admin access, encrypt data, or prevent access.

questions.questionText: What additional governance capabilities do Managed Environments provide in Power Platform?
options: A. No additional capabilities over standard environments.
B. Usage insights, maker welcome content, solution checker enforcement, and sharing limits.
C. Free unlimited storage.
D. Automatic code generation.
questions.explanation: Managed Environments provide enhanced governance features including weekly usage insights, customizable maker welcome content, solution checker enforcement for solutions, sharing limits, and data policy management. They add significant capabilities. They do not provide free storage or auto-generate code.

questions.questionText: Which two best practices should a Solution Architect follow when designing the solution architecture for ALM? (Choose two.)
options: A. Use a single solution for the entire organization's customizations.
B. Segment solutions by functional area or team to reduce deployment conflicts.
C. Use a dedicated publisher with a meaningful prefix for all custom solutions.
D. Use the default publisher for all customizations.
questions.explanation: Segmenting solutions by functional area reduces conflicts and allows independent deployment cycles. A dedicated publisher with a meaningful prefix ensures proper namespacing. A single monolithic solution creates deployment bottlenecks. The default publisher should be avoided as its prefix is generic.

questions.questionText: When designing a Power Platform solution that must scale to millions of records, which data storage approach should the architect consider?
options: A. Store all data in SharePoint lists.
B. Use Dataverse with appropriate indexing, and consider Azure SQL or data lake for archival.
C. Store data in Excel files.
D. Use only local variables in canvas apps.
questions.explanation: Dataverse with proper indexing handles large datasets efficiently, and Azure SQL or data lakes can be used for archival or heavy analytical workloads. SharePoint lists have row limits. Excel files are not suitable for millions of records. Local variables are ephemeral and limited.

questions.questionText: When would a Solution Architect recommend using Azure Service Bus for Power Platform integration?
options: A. For simple, direct point-to-point integrations.
B. When asynchronous, reliable message-based communication is needed between systems with decoupled producers and consumers.
C. For real-time video streaming.
D. For hosting Power BI reports.
questions.explanation: Azure Service Bus provides reliable, asynchronous message queuing that decouples systems, handles spikes, and ensures message delivery with features like dead-letter queues. Simple point-to-point may not need a message broker. Service Bus is not for video streaming or hosting reports.

questions.questionText: How do you restrict access to sensitive columns (fields) in a Dataverse table so only specific users can see them?
options: A. By hiding the column in the form.
B. By configuring field security profiles and adding users or teams to the profile.
C. By renaming the column.
D. By storing sensitive data in a separate environment.
questions.explanation: Field security profiles control read, create, and update access to secured columns, and users or teams are assigned to profiles to grant access. Hiding columns on forms does not secure the data (it is still accessible via API). Renaming does not secure. Separate environments add unnecessary complexity.

questions.questionText: Which CoE Starter Kit component provides visibility into all apps, flows, and makers across the tenant?
options: A. Core Components (inventory and environment details).
B. Innovation Backlog.
C. Nurture Components only.
D. Theme Builder.
questions.explanation: The Core Components of the CoE Starter Kit sync environment and resource data, providing a comprehensive inventory of all apps, flows, connectors, and makers across the tenant. Innovation Backlog is for idea management. Nurture Components support adoption. Theme Builder is for branding.

questions.questionText: Why should a Solution Architect limit the use of the default environment in an organization?
options: A. The default environment has better performance.
B. The default environment is shared by all users, making governance difficult and increasing the risk of uncontrolled growth.
C. The default environment costs extra.
D. The default environment cannot store any data.
questions.explanation: The default environment is automatically accessible to all licensed users in the organization, making it difficult to govern and prone to uncontrolled proliferation of apps and flows. Dedicated environments provide better isolation and governance. It does not have better performance. It has no extra cost. It can store data.

questions.questionText: When designing the automation strategy, which approach should the Solution Architect take for selecting between cloud flows, desktop flows, and business rules?
options: A. Use desktop flows for everything.

B. Match each automation requirement to the most appropriate technology based on the scenario: business rules for form logic, cloud flows for cloud integration, desktop flows for legacy UI automation.
C. Avoid all automation.
D. Use only Power Automate cloud flows for every scenario.
questions.explanation: The architect should evaluate each requirement and match it to the best technology: business rules for simple form-level validation, cloud flows for cloud-based orchestration and integration, and desktop flows for automating legacy applications. Using a single technology for everything is not optimal.

questions.questionText: Which two APIs does Dataverse provide for external system integration? (Choose two.)
options: A. Web API (OData v4 RESTful API).
B. Organization Service (SOAP-based SDK).
C. MQTT protocol.
D. GraphQL native endpoint.
questions.explanation: Dataverse provides the Web API (RESTful OData v4) for modern integrations and the Organization Service (SOAP-based SDK) for .NET applications. MQTT is an IoT protocol not natively provided by Dataverse. GraphQL is not a native Dataverse endpoint.

questions.questionText: What do environment groups enable in Power Platform governance?
options: A. Grouping environments for bulk DLP policy application and management.
B. Merging environments into one.
C. Deleting environments automatically.
D. Converting environments to Azure subscriptions.
questions.explanation: Environment groups allow administrators to organize environments and apply DLP policies, rules, and management operations to the group rather than individually. They do not merge, delete, or convert environments.

questions.questionText: How do business units affect data access in Dataverse?
options: A. Business units have no effect on security.
B. Business units create organizational boundaries that restrict record visibility based on user membership and security role access levels.
C. Business units only affect dashboard layouts.
D. Business units determine the number of licenses needed.
questions.explanation: Business units create hierarchical organizational boundaries. Security roles are granted at specific access levels (User, Business Unit, Parent-Child Business Units, Organization), controlling which records users can see based on their business unit membership. They significantly affect security, not layouts or licensing.

questions.questionText: Which tool or extension is commonly used in Azure DevOps pipelines to automate Power Platform solution deployment?
options: A. Visual Studio Test runner.
B. Power Platform Build Tools extension for Azure DevOps.
C. Azure DevOps Wiki.
D. Azure Boards work items.
questions.explanation: The Power Platform Build Tools extension provides pipeline tasks for exporting, importing, packing, unpacking, and managing Power Platform solutions in Azure DevOps CI/CD pipelines. VS Test runner is for testing. Wiki is for documentation. Boards is for work tracking.

questions.questionText: Which tool is recommended for bulk data migration into Dataverse when migrating from a legacy system?
options: A. Power Automate cloud flows for millions of records.
B. Dataverse import wizard for simple scenarios or tools like Azure Data Factory for complex migrations.
C. Copy-pasting from Excel.
D. Manually creating each record.
questions.explanation: For simple migrations, the Dataverse import wizard handles CSV/Excel imports. For complex, large-scale migrations, Azure Data Factory with the Dataverse connector provides robust ETL capabilities. Cloud flows have throttling limits for bulk operations. Manual methods are not feasible at scale.

questions.questionText: What is dual-write in the context of Dynamics 365 and Dataverse integration?
options: A. Writing data to two databases manually.
B. A near real-time bidirectional synchronization between Dynamics 365 Finance and Operations apps and Dataverse.
C. A backup replication strategy.
D. A two-factor authentication method.
questions.explanation: Dual-write provides near real-time, bidirectional data synchronization between Dynamics 365 Finance and Operations apps and Dataverse, enabling seamless data flow between CE and FO apps. It is not manual, not a backup strategy, and not an authentication method.

questions.questionText: What does Power Platform tenant isolation control?
options: A. Which users can log into Microsoft 365.
B. Inbound and outbound connector traffic between your tenant and other Microsoft tenants.
C. The color theme of the admin portal.
D. The number of environments allowed.
questions.explanation: Tenant isolation restricts Power Platform connectors from establishing connections to external tenants (outbound) and from external tenants connecting to yours (inbound). It does not control M365 login, portal themes, or environment limits.

questions.questionText: How does the CoE Starter Kit help with compliance and cleanup of unused resources?
options: A. It automatically deletes all apps after 30 days.
B. It provides flows that identify unused apps and flows, notifying makers and requesting justification or archival.
C. It generates compliance certificates.
D. It disables all non-admin accounts.
questions.explanation: The CoE Starter Kit includes compliance flows that identify apps and flows not used for a specified period, then contact makers to justify continued use or archive/delete them. It does not auto-delete everything, generate certificates, or disable accounts.

questions.questionText: What is the key difference between a sandbox environment and a production environment in Power Platform?
options: A. Sandbox environments have better performance.
B. Sandbox environments can be reset (data deleted) while production environments cannot be easily reset.
C. Production environments cannot store data.
D. There is no difference.
questions.explanation: Sandbox environments support reset operations that clear all data and customizations, making them ideal for testing and development. Production environments do not support easy reset to protect business data. Performance is comparable. Both store data. They have distinct differences.

questions.questionText: Which two design decisions help optimize the performance of a model-driven app? (Choose two.)
options: A. Limiting the number of columns displayed on forms and views.
B. Using appropriate indexes on frequently queried columns in Dataverse.
C. Loading all related records on every form.
D. Using synchronous plug-ins for all business logic.
questions.explanation: Reducing the number of columns on forms and views decreases data transfer and rendering time. Proper indexes speed up queries on frequently filtered columns. Loading all related records increases load time. Excessive synchronous plug-ins slow down save operations.

questions.topic: Integration Patterns - Power Automate vs Logic Apps
questions.questionText: When should a Solution Architect recommend Azure Logic Apps over Power Automate for integration?
options: A. For citizen developer scenarios.
B. When the integration requires advanced enterprise features like B2B/EDI, custom hosting, and developer-centric management in Azure.
C. For simple approval workflows.
D. Logic Apps and Power Automate are identical.
questions.explanation: Azure Logic Apps are preferred for advanced enterprise integration scenarios requiring B2B/EDI capabilities, ISE (Integration Service Environment), developer-centric Azure management, and custom hosting. Power Automate is better for citizen developers and standard business workflows. They share the same connector ecosystem but have different management and hosting models.

questions.questionText: What security consideration should an architect address when a canvas app is shared with the entire organization?
options: A. No security concerns; sharing is always safe.
B. Ensure that the underlying data connections respect proper security boundaries and that the app does not expose sensitive data through its connections.
C. Canvas apps cannot be shared.
D. Sharing automatically encrypts all data.
questions.explanation: When sharing canvas apps broadly, the architect must ensure data connections use delegated authentication or appropriate service accounts, and that the app logic does not inadvertently expose sensitive data. Sharing always has security implications. Canvas apps can be shared. Sharing does not auto-encrypt.

questions.questionText: Why should an organization implement a request and approval process for creating new Power Platform environments?
options: A. It is technically required by the platform.
B. To control environment sprawl, ensure proper governance, track resource usage, and align environments with business needs.
C. To slow down innovation.
D. Environments are free and unlimited.
questions.explanation: A request and approval process prevents environment sprawl, ensures environments are properly configured with appropriate DLP policies, tracks storage and license consumption, and aligns with IT governance requirements. It is not technically required but is a best practice. The goal is not to slow innovation but to govern it. Environments consume storage capacity.

questions.questionText: What testing approach should a Solution Architect recommend for Power Platform solutions?
options: A. No testing is needed for low-code solutions.
B. A layered approach including unit testing of business logic, integration testing, UI testing with tools like EasyRepro, and user acceptance testing.
C. Only user acceptance testing.
D. Testing only in production.
questions.explanation: A comprehensive testing strategy includes unit testing of custom code and business logic, integration testing of connectors and flows, automated UI testing, and user acceptance testing. Low-code solutions still need testing. UAT alone is insufficient. Testing in production is risky.

questions.topic: Solution Design - Mixed Reality and AI
questions.questionText: When should a Solution Architect consider incorporating AI Builder into a Power Platform solution?
options: A. For all solutions regardless of requirements.
B. When the solution requires AI capabilities like document processing, prediction, object detection, or text analysis.
C. Only for manufacturing solutions.
D. AI Builder cannot be used in Power Platform.
questions.explanation: AI Builder should be incorporated when specific AI capabilities are needed such as form processing, receipt scanning, prediction models, object detection, or sentiment analysis. It is not needed for all solutions. It works across industries. It is a native Power Platform capability.

questions.questionText: When is an on-premises data gateway required for Power Platform?
options: A. For all Power Platform connections.
B. When connecting to on-premises data sources like SQL Server, file shares, or other systems not accessible from the cloud.
C. For connecting to Microsoft 365 services.
D. When using premium connectors.
questions.explanation: The on-premises data gateway is required when Power Platform needs to access data sources that are behind a firewall or on-premises network (SQL Server, Oracle, file shares, etc.). Cloud-to-cloud connections do not need it. M365 services are cloud-based. Premium connectors may or may not need a gateway depending on the source.

questions.questionText: How can Microsoft Entra Conditional Access policies enhance Power Platform security?
options: A. By controlling which colors the apps display.
B. By enforcing conditions like MFA, device compliance, and location restrictions before users can access Power Platform services.
C. By automatically building apps.
D. By replacing Dataverse security roles.
questions.explanation: Conditional Access policies can require multi-factor authentication, enforce device compliance, restrict access by location or risk level, and more before allowing access to Power Platform. They do not control visual styling, build apps, or replace Dataverse security (they complement it).

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of the Nurture Components in the CoE Starter Kit?
options: A. To block all maker activity.
B. To foster community engagement, provide training resources, and support citizen developer growth.
C. To delete unused environments.
D. To generate billing invoices.
questions.explanation: Nurture Components provide tools for community engagement like a training portal, maker showcase app, and communication templates that support citizen developer growth and adoption. They do not block activity, delete environments, or generate invoices.

questions.questionText: What types of capacity should a Solution Architect monitor for Power Platform environments?
options: A. Only file storage.
B. Database capacity (Dataverse), file capacity, and log capacity across environments.
C. Only the number of users.
D. CPU and memory of the server hardware.
questions.explanation: Power Platform capacity includes Dataverse database storage, file storage (attachments, images), and log storage (audit logs, plug-in trace logs) that must be monitored across all environments. It is not just file storage or user count. Cloud platform capacity is managed by Microsoft, not hardware.

questions.questionText: When designing a Power Platform solution that must work offline for field workers, which approach should the architect recommend?
options: A. Use only cloud flows for all operations.
B. Configure the model-driven app's mobile offline profile with the appropriate tables and filters for offline access.
C. Download data to Excel manually.
D. Offline is not supported in Power Platform.
questions.explanation: Model-driven apps support mobile offline profiles that define which tables and records are available offline, enabling field workers to access and modify data without connectivity. Cloud flows require connectivity. Manual Excel download is not integrated. Offline is supported in Power Platform.

questions.questionText: Which two areas does Power Platform Advisor provide recommendations on? (Choose two.)
options: A. Security best practices and policy compliance.
B. Performance optimization and solution health.
C. Office furniture arrangement.
D. Employee vacation scheduling.
questions.explanation: Power Platform Advisor analyzes your environment and provides recommendations on security, compliance, performance, and solution health to help maintain a well-governed and optimized platform. It does not cover physical office setup or HR scheduling.

questions.questionText: What should a Solution Architecture Document (SAD) for a Power Platform project include?
options: A. Only the list of required licenses.
B. Business requirements, solution overview, data model, security design, integration architecture, ALM strategy, and non-functional requirements.
C. Only screenshots of the finished application.
D. Only the project timeline.
questions.explanation: A comprehensive SAD covers business requirements, solution overview with component diagrams, data model design, security architecture, integration patterns, ALM and deployment strategy, and non-functional requirements like performance and scalability. Licenses, screenshots, and timelines are supporting details but not the complete document.

questions.questionText: During a fit/gap analysis for a Power Platform solution, the architect identifies a requirement that cannot be met by out-of-the-box Dataverse or Dynamics 365 features. What should the architect do next?
options: A. Remove the requirement from scope.
B. Evaluate AppSource solutions, custom development, or third-party integrations to address the gap.
C. Tell the customer the requirement is impossible.
D. Switch to a completely different platform.
questions.explanation: When a gap is identified, the architect should evaluate alternative approaches such as AppSource marketplace solutions, custom Power Platform development (plug-ins, PCF controls, custom connectors), or third-party integrations. Removing the requirement (A) ignores a valid business need. Declaring it impossible (C) is premature without exploring alternatives. Switching platforms (D) is disproportionate when alternatives exist within the ecosystem.

questions.questionText: Which two factors should a Solution Architect assess when evaluating an organization's risk factors during solution planning? (Choose two.)
options: A. The organization's change management readiness and user adoption maturity.
B. The complexity of existing integrations and data migration requirements.
C. The color scheme of the organization's logo.
D. The personal preferences of the project sponsor.
questions.explanation: Change management readiness (A) directly impacts whether users will adopt the new solution, and integration/migration complexity (B) affects timeline, cost, and technical risk. Logo colors (C) are irrelevant to solution risk. Personal preferences (D) should not drive risk assessment decisions.

questions.questionText: An organization has operations in three countries with different data residency requirements. How should the Solution Architect design the environment topology?
options: A. Use a single global environment for all countries.
B. Deploy separate environments in the appropriate geographic regions to comply with data residency requirements.
C. Store all data in a single Azure Blob Storage account.
D. Use only SharePoint for data storage to avoid residency issues.
questions.explanation: Separate environments in the appropriate geographic regions ensure compliance with data residency regulations such as GDPR in Europe. Each Dataverse environment is provisioned in a specific region. A single global environment (A) may violate data residency requirements. Azure Blob Storage (C) does not replace Dataverse. SharePoint (D) does not solve data residency for Power Platform data.

questions.topic: Architect a Solution - Data Model Design
questions.questionText: When designing the Dataverse data model, a Solution Architect needs to represent a many-to-many relationship between Contacts and Projects. What is the recommended approach?
options: A. Create a lookup column on the Contact table pointing to Project.
B. Use a native many-to-many (N:N) relationship or create an intersect table if additional attributes are needed on the relationship.
C. Store project IDs as a comma-separated string in a text column on Contact.
D. Create duplicate Contact records for each Project.
questions.explanation: Dataverse supports native N:N relationships for simple associations, or a custom intersect table when additional attributes (like role, start date) are needed on the relationship. A single lookup (A) only supports one-to-many. Comma-separated strings (C) violate relational design principles and prevent proper querying. Duplicate records (D) create data integrity issues.

questions.questionText: A Solution Architect is designing a parent-child relationship between an Account table and a custom Project table. The business requires that when an Account is deleted, all related Projects must also be deleted. Which relationship behavior should be configured?
options: A. Referential relationship behavior.
B. Parental (cascade delete) relationship behavior.
C. Custom relationship with no cascade.
D. Configurable cascade behavior set to Remove Link.
questions.explanation: Parental relationship behavior cascades the delete action from parent to child records, automatically deleting all related Projects when the Account is deleted. Referential (A) only removes the link but does not delete child records. No cascade (C) leaves orphaned child records. Remove Link (D) clears the lookup value but does not delete the child records.

questions.questionText: An external system needs to call the Dataverse Web API without a user context. What authentication approach should the Solution Architect recommend?
options: A. Store a user's username and password in the external system.
B. Register an application in Microsoft Entra ID and use OAuth 2.0 client credentials (application user) for server-to-server authentication.
C. Use anonymous access to the Web API.
D. Share a personal access token via email.
questions.explanation: Server-to-server (S2S) authentication using an Entra ID app registration with OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow is the recommended approach for non-interactive integrations. The application user is assigned a security role in Dataverse to control access. Storing credentials (A) is a security risk. Anonymous access (C) is not supported by Dataverse. Sharing tokens via email (D) is insecure and tokens expire.

questions.questionText: A customer requires a disaster recovery strategy for their Power Platform solution. What should the Solution Architect include in the business continuity plan?
options: A. Rely solely on Microsoft's built-in redundancy with no additional planning.
B. Define backup and restore procedures, document environment replication strategies, establish RPO/RTO targets, and plan for failover scenarios.
C. Keep a printed copy of all data.
D. Use only a single environment to simplify recovery.
questions.explanation: A proper business continuity plan includes defined backup procedures (system and manual backups), environment replication across regions if needed, Recovery Point Objective (RPO) and Recovery Time Objective (RTO) targets, and documented failover procedures. While Microsoft provides infrastructure redundancy, organizations must plan for application-level recovery (A is insufficient). Printed copies (C) are impractical. A single environment (D) creates a single point of failure.

questions.questionText: An organization needs to integrate Power Automate with a proprietary REST API that does not have a standard connector. What should the Solution Architect recommend?
options: A. Abandon the integration requirement.
B. Build a custom connector that defines the API endpoints, authentication, and data schema.
C. Manually copy data between systems.
D. Rebuild the proprietary system in Power Apps.
questions.explanation: Custom connectors allow Power Platform to connect to any REST API by defining the API endpoints, authentication methods (OAuth 2.0, API key, Basic), and request/response schemas. This enables Power Automate flows and Power Apps to interact with the API. Abandoning the requirement (A) ignores the business need. Manual copying (C) is error-prone and not scalable. Rebuilding (D) is unnecessarily costly.

questions.questionText: When should a Solution Architect recommend Power Automate desktop flows (RPA) as part of the solution?
options: A. For all automation scenarios regardless of API availability.
B. When automating legacy systems that lack APIs or modern integration capabilities, requiring UI-based interaction.
C. As a replacement for all cloud flows.
D. Only for testing purposes.
questions.explanation: Desktop flows (RPA) are specifically designed for automating legacy applications that do not have APIs or modern integration capabilities, using UI-based interactions like mouse clicks and keyboard input. API-based integration should be preferred when available (A is incorrect). Desktop flows complement cloud flows, they do not replace them (C). They are production tools, not just for testing (D).

questions.questionText: A Solution Architect needs to provide external partners with access to specific Dataverse data. What is the recommended approach?
options: A. Give external partners full internal user accounts.
B. Use Power Pages (portals) with appropriate web roles and table permissions to provide controlled external access.
C. Email data exports to partners weekly.
D. Disable all security for the environment.
questions.explanation: Power Pages (formerly Power Apps portals) provide authenticated external access to Dataverse data with web roles and table permissions controlling what external users can see and do. Full internal accounts (A) are costly and over-privileged. Email exports (C) are not real-time and risk data leakage. Disabling security (D) exposes all data to everyone.

questions.questionText: Which two benefits does using Microsoft Entra ID security groups with Dataverse provide? (Choose two.)
options: A. Automatically assigning security roles to users based on group membership.
B. Restricting environment access to only members of a specific security group.
C. Eliminating the need for Dataverse security roles entirely.
D. Automatically creating Dataverse tables.
questions.explanation: Entra ID security groups can be associated with Dataverse environments to restrict access (B), and Dataverse teams linked to Entra ID groups can automatically assign security roles (A) when users are added to the group. Entra ID groups do not replace security roles (C); they work together. Groups do not create tables (D).

questions.questionText: A Solution Architect discovers that a Power Automate flow makes 15,000 Dataverse API calls per minute during a batch process. What concern should this raise?
options: A. No concern; there are no API limits.
B. The flow may exceed Dataverse API request limits, resulting in 429 throttling errors and degraded performance for all users.
C. API calls are free and unlimited for all license types.
D. Only custom code is subject to API limits, not flows.
questions.explanation: Dataverse enforces API request limits per user and per environment to ensure fair resource usage. Exceeding these limits triggers HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) responses and can degrade performance for all users in the environment. API limits exist (A is wrong). Limits depend on license type (C is wrong). Both flows and custom code are subject to limits (D is wrong). The architect should implement retry logic, batch operations, or spread processing over time.

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of the Solution Checker in Power Platform?
options: A. To check if solutions have been purchased.
B. To perform static analysis of Power Platform solutions against best practice rules, identifying performance issues, reliability risks, and security concerns.
C. To count the number of components in a solution.
D. To automatically fix all issues in a solution.
questions.explanation: Solution Checker analyzes customizations against a set of best practice rules covering performance, reliability, security, accessibility, and maintainability. It identifies issues like inefficient plug-in code, deprecated API usage, and potential performance problems. It does not check purchases (A), merely count components (C), or auto-fix issues (D); it reports findings for developers to address.

questions.questionText: Which two factors should a Solution Architect verify as part of go-live readiness? (Choose two.)
options: A. All user acceptance testing has been completed and signed off.
B. Data migration has been validated with reconciliation reports confirming accuracy.
C. The office kitchen has been restocked.
D. All developers have updated their LinkedIn profiles.
questions.explanation: UAT sign-off (A) confirms the solution meets business requirements from the users' perspective. Data migration validation (B) ensures migrated data is accurate, complete, and reconciled against source systems. Office kitchen (C) and LinkedIn profiles (D) are irrelevant to solution go-live readiness.

questions.questionText: Users report that a model-driven app form takes over 10 seconds to load. What should the Solution Architect investigate first?
options: A. The users' monitor resolution.
B. The number of synchronous plug-ins, sub-grids, embedded canvas apps, and custom JavaScript executing on form load.
C. The version of Microsoft Word installed.
D. The users' email signature length.
questions.explanation: Form load performance is directly affected by synchronous plug-ins that execute during record retrieval, the number of sub-grids loading data, embedded canvas apps initializing, and custom JavaScript running on form load. Reducing these improves load time. Monitor resolution (A), Word version (C), and email signatures (D) do not affect model-driven app form load performance.

questions.topic: Architect a Solution - Data Visualization Strategy
questions.questionText: An organization needs executive dashboards showing aggregated data from multiple Dataverse tables and external data sources. Which approach should the Solution Architect recommend?
options: A. Build all dashboards as canvas apps.
B. Use Power BI embedded in model-driven apps for rich, interactive dashboards combining multiple data sources.
C. Export data to Excel and create manual charts.
D. Use only Dataverse system dashboards for all reporting needs.
questions.explanation: Power BI provides rich, interactive dashboards that can combine data from Dataverse, SQL, and other sources, and can be embedded in model-driven apps for seamless access. Canvas apps (A) are not optimized for complex data visualization. Manual Excel charts (C) are not real-time or scalable. System dashboards (D) have limited visualization capabilities and cannot easily combine external data sources.

questions.questionText: A customer wants to store large documents (contracts, drawings) associated with Dataverse records without consuming Dataverse database storage. What should the Solution Architect recommend?
options: A. Store documents as Dataverse notes with attachments.
B. Enable SharePoint integration for document management, which stores files in SharePoint while maintaining the association with Dataverse records.
C. Store documents as base64-encoded text in a Dataverse column.
D. Use Azure Blob Storage with no connection to Dataverse.
questions.explanation: SharePoint integration for Dataverse stores documents in SharePoint document libraries while maintaining navigation and association from the Dataverse record. This avoids consuming Dataverse database storage capacity for large files. Dataverse notes (A) consume database storage. Base64 encoding (C) dramatically increases storage usage and is impractical. Azure Blob Storage (D) works but lacks the built-in seamless integration that SharePoint provides with Dataverse.

questions.questionText: How can a Solution Architect leverage Microsoft Teams integration with Power Platform for collaborative scenarios?
options: A. Teams cannot integrate with Power Platform.
B. Embed model-driven or canvas apps as tabs in Teams channels, use adaptive cards in Teams from Power Automate, and leverage Dataverse for Teams for lightweight scenarios.
C. Only use Teams for voice calls.
D. Replace all Power Platform apps with Teams apps.
questions.explanation: Teams integration includes embedding Power Apps as tabs, sending adaptive cards and approval notifications via Power Automate, using Dataverse for Teams for team-specific lightweight apps, and leveraging Power Virtual Agents (Copilot Studio) chatbots in Teams. Teams does integrate with Power Platform (A is wrong). Teams supports much more than voice calls (C). Teams complements Power Platform rather than replacing it (D).

questions.topic: Solution Envisioning - ISV and AppSource Evaluation
questions.questionText: When evaluating an AppSource solution to address a functional requirement, what should the Solution Architect consider?
options: A. Only the price of the solution.
B. The solution's compatibility with existing customizations, its vendor support model, update frequency, licensing impact, and alignment with the overall solution architecture.
C. Whether the solution has the most downloads.
D. Only whether it has good reviews.
questions.explanation: A thorough evaluation considers compatibility with existing customizations, vendor support and roadmap, update frequency and ALM impact, licensing costs and model, security implications, and how well it fits the overall architecture. Price alone (A) ignores compatibility and support risks. Download count (C) does not guarantee fit. Reviews (D) are one input but not sufficient for an architectural decision.

questions.questionText: An enterprise has multiple development teams working on different Power Platform solutions. How should the Solution Architect structure development environments?
options: A. All teams share a single development environment.
B. Assign separate development environments per team or project to isolate development activities and reduce conflicts.
C. Use only the default environment for all development.
D. Each developer should use their own production environment.
questions.explanation: Separate development environments per team or project prevent conflicts from overlapping customizations, allow independent development cycles, and support proper solution segmentation. Sharing a single environment (A) causes conflicts and makes it difficult to isolate changes. The default environment (C) should not be used for development. Production environments (D) should never be used for development.

questions.questionText: Which two components should be included in a Power Platform solution deployment plan? (Choose two.)
options: A. A sequence of managed solution imports with dependency order documented.
B. A rollback procedure in case the deployment fails.
C. A plan for the team holiday party.
D. A list of the team's favorite restaurants.
questions.explanation: Deployment plans must include the ordered sequence of solution imports respecting dependencies (A) and a documented rollback procedure to revert to the previous state if issues are discovered (B). Social activities (C) and restaurant preferences (D) are not deployment planning concerns.

questions.topic: Architect a Solution - Networking and ExpressRoute
questions.questionText: An organization with strict network security requirements wants to ensure that traffic between their on-premises network and Power Platform does not traverse the public internet. What should the Solution Architect recommend?
options: A. Use a VPN tunnel to each Power Platform service individually.
B. Implement Azure ExpressRoute with Microsoft peering to provide a private connection path for Power Platform traffic.
C. Disable all internet access in the organization.
D. Use only mobile data connections.
questions.explanation: Azure ExpressRoute with Microsoft peering provides a private, dedicated connection between on-premises networks and Microsoft cloud services including Power Platform, avoiding the public internet. Individual VPN tunnels (A) are not how Power Platform connectivity works. Disabling internet (C) would prevent access to cloud services entirely. Mobile data (D) still traverses the public internet.

questions.topic: Architect a Solution - Power Platform Well-Architected Framework
questions.questionText: What is the Power Platform Well-Architected Framework, and how should a Solution Architect use it?
options: A. A framework for building physical architecture diagrams of office buildings.

B. A set of best practices and guiding tenets across pillars including reliability, security, operational excellence, performance efficiency, and experience optimization that the architect should apply to solution design decisions.
C. A tool for generating code automatically.
D. A licensing calculator for Power Platform.
questions.explanation: The Power Platform Well-Architected Framework provides design principles and best practices organized into pillars (reliability, security, operational excellence, performance efficiency, and experience optimization) that guide architectural decisions. It is not about physical buildings (A), code generation (C), or licensing (D). Solution Architects should evaluate their designs against these pillars to ensure robust, well-designed solutions.
